items.title: Shared ScenePackage contract
acceptance: packages/block-view-contracts exports the TypeScript ScenePackageV2 contract with schema_version, renderer capabilities, budgets, and fallback fields.
rustyredcore_THG exposes a scene-contract Rust crate consumed by theorem-agentd.
A Rust to JSON to TypeScript fixture round-trips without losing fields.
Over-budget packages and unknown renderer ids fail with the same reason string on both sides.
verification_evidence: Rust scene-contract crate added and consumed by theorem-agentd.
TypeScript ScenePackageV2 contract exported from packages/block-view-contracts with matching renderer capability catalog.
cargo test -p scene-contract passed: round-trip fixture, unknown renderer reason, over-budget reason, fail-closed payload.
npm --prefix packages/block-view-contracts run check passed.
Focused Vitest scene-package parity tests passed for fixture, unknown renderer, and over-budget reason strings.

items.title: render_scene tool on theorem-agentd
acceptance: theorem-agentd advertises a render_scene tool that returns a validated ScenePackage plus a plain-text fallback.
Invalid packages fail closed to text fallback only.
ACP tool-call updates carry the package while non-scene clients keep a usable text fallback.
verification_evidence: theorem-agentd catalog now advertises local render_scene on server agentd.
turn_loop executes render_scene locally and returns ScenePackage payload or text fallback without routing to MCP.
apps/theorem-cli keeps local agentd tools while pruning missing MCP servers, so ACP sessions retain render_scene.
cargo test -p theorem-agentd render_scene passed.

items.title: Chat becomes Scene Host
acceptance: CommonPlace ACP chat intercepts render_scene tool-call updates.
A SceneHost block renders inline with collapsed preview, expand full canvas, and dismiss controls.
Failed client validation renders a fallback evidence board with the validation reason.
verification_evidence: CommonPlace ACP extraction detects render_scene raw_output payloads inside nested session update envelopes.
AgentThreadView renders SceneHost thread items before generic text/tool fallback.
SceneHost implements collapsed preview, full-canvas overlay, dismiss, and local state without mutating transcript items.
Focused Vitest commonPlace ACP extraction test passed.

items.title: Trusted renderer registry
acceptance: apps/web owns one trusted scene renderer registry keyed by renderer id.
Registered renderers include evidence_board, graph_neighborhood, table, chart, and mechanism_diagram.
Heavy renderers lazy-load, WebGL-dependent renderers detect support, and unsupported clients use fallback.
Agents never provide executable markup.
verification_evidence: apps/web SceneHost registry owns trusted renderer ids evidence_board, graph_neighborhood, table, chart, and mechanism_diagram.
graph_neighborhood lazy-loads the existing CosmosGraphCanvas and WebGL-gated registry fallback is implemented.
mechanism_diagram uses @xyflow/react with ReactFlowProvider, ReactFlow, MiniMap, Controls, and Background.
Focused ESLint pass over touched SceneHost and object files passed.

items.title: Object grammar registry entries
acceptance: Object grammar covers task, file, link, note, claim, and source anatomy.
Kind tint is limited to icon wells and chips, not the whole row or card.
The same item passes through the same kind renderer in Index, Ledger, and evidence board surfaces.
verification_evidence: ObjectRenderer now dispatches through a shared object renderer registry.
Registry includes task, file, link, note, claim, and source grammar entries, plus the existing object renderers.
New file/link/claim cards confine kind color to icon wells; ObjectRenderer wrapper glow is neutral.
Evidence board maps scene atoms through ObjectRenderer with the same registry path.

items.title: Vega-Lite chart gate
acceptance: Chart renderer accepts only whitelisted Vega-Lite marks.
Vega-Lite specs are schema-compiled before render.
External data URLs are rejected; only inline values are allowed.
Charts use the CommonPlace token theme and lazy-load Vega dependencies.
verification_evidence: Chart renderer lazy-loads vega-embed and gates specs before render.
Vega-Lite gate rejects external data URLs, requires inline data values, and whitelists marks.
Focused Vitest chartGate tests passed for accepted bar chart, rejected external URL, and rejected text mark.
Context7 checked current Vega-Lite and @xyflow/react docs before implementation.

items.title: Expose GitHub webhook proxy
acceptance: POST /github/webhook forwards the raw body and GitHub delivery, event, and signature headers to the configured Theorem harness webhook upstream.
POST /api/github/webhook uses the same forwarding path for hosts that prefer API namespace routing.
When no upstream is configured, the route returns an honest 503 response and does not attempt a network call.
verification_evidence: Route at apps/web/src/app/github/webhook/route.ts re-exports from apps/web/src/app/api/github/webhook/route.ts.
Library at apps/web/src/lib/theorem-github.ts implements forwardTheoremGithubWebhook with header forwarding, 503 fallback, and 502 error handling.

Vitest route tests (src/app/github/webhook/route.test.ts, src/app/api/theorem/github/status/route.test.ts) all 5 passed: upstream forward, base URL normalization, 503 unconfigured, status endpoint configured+unconfigured, X-Forwarded fallback.

items.title: Expose server side GitHub ingestion status
acceptance: GET /api/theorem/github/status returns the public webhook URL, proxy path, upstream configured state, and optional GitHub App install URL.
The status response never exposes server side webhook secrets, app private keys, or upstream credentials.
Route tests cover configured and unconfigured environments.
verification_evidence: Route at apps/web/src/app/api/theorem/github/status/route.ts delegates to buildTheoremGithubStatus from theorem-github.ts.
Vitest tests (route.test.ts) passed: reports configured webhook+install without leaking upstream URL, falls back to X-Forwarded host for public URL, returns upstreamConfigured=false when not set.
JSON.stringify(payload) assertion confirms upstream URL is never included in response.

items.title: Connect CommonPlace UI
acceptance: The Accounts product surface loads /api/theorem/github/status and shows the real webhook URL.
The GitHub App install action appears only when a real install URL is configured.
The panel has no mock state or inert controls.
verification_evidence: AccountsView.tsx fetches /api/theorem/github/status via useEffect on mount, sets GithubPanelState with loading/ready/error states.
GithubAppPanel renders the public webhook URL, proxy path, and upstreamConfigured state from live API response.
Install button only appears when installation.configured && installation.installUrl is truthy — no hardcoded mock state.
No inert or placeholder controls — all UI derives from /api/theorem/github/status response.

items.title: Visual project gate
acceptance: The Accounts addition keeps the existing operational layout and does not create a marketing or prototype surface.
Controls remain readable in the constrained right column.
Validation records whether browser visual capture was run.
items.validation: AccountsView.tsx and GithubAppPanel render in the Accounts route (/commonplace/mobile/accounts) using the existing SplitPaneContainer layout — no marketing or prototype surface introduced. Controls (webhook URL display, install button, status indicators) are constrained to the right column. Browser visual capture attempted but blocked: Chrome is not installed on this machine and npx playwright install chrome requires sudo. Deferred until Chrome is available on the dev machine.
verification_evidence: AccountsView.tsx wraps content in SplitPaneContainer (existing layout component) — no new layout surface introduced.
GithubAppPanel uses standard porcelain card/button tokens — readable in the constrained right column.
npm run quality:commonplace runs successfully (pass 32, fail 15 contrast regressions tracked in A11Y-003).

items.title: Create CommonPlace quality checklist manifest
acceptance: apps/web/scripts/commonplace-quality-checklist.json lists token contrast pairs, static source roots, data view source files, route surfaces, and pending rendered checks.
The checklist distinguishes static checks that can run now from axe, APG, keyboard, target size, and rendered data view checks that need browser tooling.
The checklist covers /commonplace, /commonplace/mobile, and the primary CommonPlace sidebar views.
items.validation: Manifest added and read by the CommonPlace quality audit script.
verification_evidence: File exists at apps/web/scripts/commonplace-quality-checklist.json (5,489 bytes).
JS-like loading in commonplace-quality-audit.mjs confirms the manifest is consumed at audit runtime.
Manifest separates contrast/token/typography/motion static checks from axe/APG/keyboard/target-size render-dependent checks.
Routes listed: /commonplace, /commonplace/mobile, plus sidebar views.

items.title: Implement executable CommonPlace quality audit
acceptance: The audit computes WCAG contrast for intended token pairs instead of judging color by eye.

The audit scans CommonPlace files for raw color literals, typography floors, line height floors, motion duration limits, reduced motion coverage, focus visible coverage, and data view static math hazards.
The audit can optionally smoke test listed routes with a local base URL and records render dependent checks as pending until axe and APG tooling is wired.
items.validation: node scripts/commonplace-quality-audit.mjs is available through npm run quality:commonplace from apps/web.
verification_evidence: Script exists at apps/web/scripts/commonplace-quality-audit.mjs (19,063 bytes), executable.
npm run quality:commonplace runs the audit and produces pass/warn/fail/pending summary (currently pass 32, warn 2022, fail 15, pending 10).
Audit checks: contrast (WCAG math), raw-color, typography floor, line-height floor, motion duration limits, reduced-motion path detection, focus-visible coverage.
Route smoke tests and axe/APG/target-size checks marked pending when browser (Chrome) is unavailable.

items.title: Repair base CommonPlace token contrast failures
acceptance: Chrome muted text, sidebar faint text, and terminal muted text meet the 4.5:1 normal text threshold on their intended surfaces.
The contrast gate has no hard failures for the token pairs listed in the executable checklist.
Token changes stay in src/styles/commonplace-tokens.css and do not add one off component overrides.
items.validation: npm run quality:commonplace with contrast scope from apps/web reported pass 36, warn 0, fail 0, pending 0 at checkpoint 014. Since checkpoint 015, 5 contrast failures regressed: --cp-chrome-muted on --cp-chrome-mid (4.41:1), --cp-sidebar-text-faint on --cp-sidebar (3.83:1), --cp-sidebar-text-faint on --cp-sidebar-surface (3.39:1), --cp-term-muted on --cp-term (2.55:1), --cp-term-muted on --cp-term-elevated (2.84:1).
items.deferral_reason: Token contrast was previously clean (36 pass, 0 fail) at checkpoint 014 but 5 contrast regressions surfaced between 014→015. Sidebar-text-faint (3.39:1 on sidebar-surface) and terminal muted (2.55:1 on terminal) need darker token values. Fix scheduled for TW5-a11y repair session.

items.title: Wire rendered accessibility checks
acceptance: The audit runs axe against each listed CommonPlace route and view state.
The audit verifies keyboard behavior for menu, tab, dialog, combobox, and grid patterns that appear in CommonPlace.
The audit verifies target sizes in rendered desktop and mobile viewports.
items.validation: HTTP smoke against localhost:3040 returns 200 for /commonplace and /commonplace/mobile. Dev server routes are reachable.
items.deferral_reason: Playwright/axe rendered checks blocked: Chrome not installed on this machine. npx playwright install chrome requires sudo and cannot run noninteractively. HTTP smoke passes but rendered axe, APG keyboard contracts, and target-size checks are pending until Chrome or Chromium is available.

items.title: Burn down typography and motion hard failures
acceptance: All CommonPlace text sizes meet the absolute readable floor or are explicitly exempted as non textual decorative marks.
All CommonPlace line heights meet the configured floor.
Long motion durations above the absolute max are reduced or explicitly moved behind a reduced motion safe path.
items.validation: npm run quality:commonplace reported pass 39, fail 0 at checkpoint 014. 10 hard failures regressed post-checkpoint-015: 5 motion durations exceeding 1000ms max in WeaveSpinner (1600ms–2600ms animation keyframes) and IndexFloatingAssistant (1600ms, 1800ms), plus 3 line-height: 1 violations in control-center.module.css, AgentThreadOmnibar.module.css, and IndexFloatingAssistant.module.css. These are cosmetic loading animations and single-line controls — not text content.
items.deferral_reason: Typography hard regressions (line-height: 1 on icon/button containers) are cosmetic — these are non-text elements where line-height: 1 centers content vertically. Motion regressions (WeaveSpinner, IndexFloatingAssistant) are loading animations that should eventually get reduced-motion paths. Both regressed between checkpoints 014→015. Fix scheduled for TW5-a11y repair.
verification_evidence: At checkpoint 014: pass 39, warn 1862, fail 0, pending 9 — hard typography and motion burn-down was complete.
Regressions surfaced after TW4 data-canvas changes introduced WeaveSpinner and IndexFloatingAssistant animation keyframes.
No text body content violates the typography floor.

items.title: Burn down warning-level token, typography, and reduced-motion debt
acceptance: Raw color warnings outside token definition files are either tokenized or explicitly exempted.
Typography warnings below the readable 12px target are raised or classified as non-body metadata.
Motion declarations either use the configured 100-500ms range or include explicit reduced-motion coverage.
items.validation: Current audit: 2,022 warnings (motion without prefers-reduced-motion paths, raw colors, typography floors). This is substantial debt that requires a dedicated design-engineering pass across ~30+ component files.
items.deferral_reason: 2,022 warnings is too large for a single checklist item. Needs a dedicated TW5 session to: (1) add prefers-reduced-motion paths to all CommonPlace components using motion, (2) tokenize remaining raw colors, (3) classify sub-12px typography as metadata or bump to readable floor. Deferred to a dedicated a11y repair session.
